$comment: SessionStart hook auto-injects YourScore's current state (the YOURSCORE.md 'Recently Shipped' changelog + the last 10 commits) into every session's context, so agents start current without being told. Read-only. To disable, delete the hooks block.

hooks.command: cd "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}" 2>/dev/null && { echo 'YourScore is a live product; read YOURSCORE.md before referencing features and do not claim a feature is unbuilt without checking. Current state:'; echo '--- Recently shipped (from YOURSCORE.md) ---'; awk '/^## 0\. Recently Shipped/{f=1} f{print} /^## 1\./{if(f)exit}' YOURSCORE.md 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^- \*\*20' | head -12; echo '--- Last 10 commits ---'; git log -10 --date=short --pretty='%ad %s' 2>/dev/null; } || true
